lane_verdicts.evidence: Matches SOURCE_FACT_AUTHORITY_MATRIX panel assignment authority: PANEL_ALLOCATION_SOURCE with pre-design chronology or approved deterministic assignment rule; SOURCE_ROLE_AND_CHRONOLOGY_POLICY permits PANEL_ALLOCATION_SOURCE as generator-eligible and forbids post-design/completed/unknown roles; AUTONOMOUS_EVAL_SOURCE_APPROVAL supplies deterministic validation, four-reviewer quorum, adjudication, sanitized bundle verification, and independent audit sequence.

Matches SOURCE_FACT_AUTHORITY_MATRIX footprint and mounting geometry authority: verified component library or model-specific approved rule; ONE_PROJECT_TOPOLOGY_SIZING_PLACEMENT_CALIBRATION recognizes VERIFIED_MODEL_GEOMETRY, APPROVED_GENERIC_CONSERVATIVE_ENVELOPE, GEOMETRY_MISSING, GEOMETRY_CONFLICT, and NOT_APPLICABLE states.

Matches ONE_PROJECT_TOPOLOGY_SIZING_PLACEMENT_CALIBRATION requirements for approved topology, sizing, mounting, clearance, thermal, ventilation, spare-space, fill-denominator, hard-constraint, provenance, and safe-unresolved states; proposal requires explicit human or accepted company default authority and versioned rule review.

evidence: Lane A rejects completed sheetmetal drawing, post-design allocation label, filename/worksheet-label inference, component cooccurrence, and procurement quantity.
Lane B rejects completed drawing geometry, symbol-name geometry guessing, private model public lookup, and synthetic fixture only as real coverage.
Lane C rejects historical cabinet size copy, completed-reference coordinate copy, private intuition, and soft objectives over hard constraints.
Proposal requires unresolved facts to remain UNVERIFIED, TBD, CONFLICT, or HUMAN_REVIEW_REQUIRED rather than guessed.
Panel assignment gates are covered by proposed tests for valid PANEL_ALLOCATION_SOURCE assignment path, post-design allocation label rejection, and completed-reference assignment signal rejection.

Component geometry and critical-dimension gates are covered by proposed tests for verified library geometry resolution, generic conservative envelope provenance, and geometry conflict/missing preservation.

Topology, sizing, placement, and hard-constraint gates are covered by proposed tests for standard cabinet candidate selection without exact customer copying, clearance/containment/overlap/mounting-surface validation, and zero-denominator handling.
Leakage and output-generation gates are covered by proposed tests for no customer PDF/DXF/DWG generation and no private transmission/tracked private artifacts.

Production approval and public-web prohibitions remain explicit hard boundaries in the task brief, proposal booleans, master protocol, and AGENTS.md; the authority decision packet should preserve them as non-negotiable gates for any accepted implementation.

Proposal intended_diff_scope_if_accepted is limited to docs/specs, rules/sheetmetal-v1, schemas only if required, scripts/sheetmetal_v1.py, scripts/run_tests.py, active frozen workflow manifest, neutral reports, and sheetmetal-v1 manifests.

Proposal forbidden_diff_scope excludes source roots, .private, completed references, frozen releases, frozen graders, legacy historical evidence, and scripts/run_reference_detection_v4_corpus_screening.py.

Rollback is defined as reverting accepted implementation commits and proposal-derived neutral reports, regenerating active frozen manifests from the previous accepted commit, and keeping private artifacts ignored and unstaged.
No required-check blocker remains.

Evidence supports next action PREPARE_SOURCE_RULE_AUTHORITY_DECISION_PACKET.
